name: C Major
notes: C D E F G A B
desc: The universal reference point. All natural notes with no accidentals. The foundation for understanding music theory and the easiest key for beginners learning piano.

name: G Major
notes: G A B C D E F#
desc: The gateway key to guitar playing. Open G, C, D, Em, Am chords sit naturally here. From folk ballads to modern pop, G Major radiates warmth and accessibility.

name: D Major
notes: D E F# G A B C#
desc: Brilliant and ringing on acoustic guitar thanks to open D and A strings. A staple of folk, country, and singer-songwriter music with an open, spacious quality.

name: A Major
notes: A B C# D E F# G#
desc: The sweet spot between open chords and barre technique. Popular in rock, pop, and psychedelic music. A Major has an energetic, forward-moving quality that drives modern songwriting.

name: E Major
notes: E F# G# A B C# D#
desc: The root note of standard guitar tuning. Electric blues is built on E Major — Hendrix, Clapton, and Stevie Ray Vaughan lived here. Bright, ringing, and perfect for power chords.

name: B Major
notes: B C# D# E F# G# A#
desc: Cutting and brilliant with all those sharps. Common in worship music, pop production, and modern gospel. The brightness intensifies as you move through the sharp keys.

name: F# Major
notes: F# G# A# B C# D# E#
desc: Enharmonically equivalent to Gb Major. Rarely written but theoretically important. Chopin and other classical composers occasionally ventured here for special effects.

name: F Major
notes: F G A Bb C D E
desc: Warm, grounded, and fundamental to vocal writing. R&B, soul, and jazz singers favor F Major for its resonant, rounded quality. Mozart and Beethoven wrote beautiful works here.

name: Bb Major
notes: Bb C D Eb F G A
desc: The home key of trumpets and clarinets. Most jazz standards are written in Bb Major. New Orleans jazz, big band, and brass ensemble music all gravitate here naturally.

name: Eb Major
notes: Eb F G Ab Bb C D
desc: Rich and soulful, the favorite of Motown. R&B, gospel, and jazz ballads thrive here. The flatted keys have a darker, more introspective warmth than their sharp counterparts.

name: Ab Major
notes: Ab Bb C Db Eb F G
desc: Lush and voluminous. Neo-soul, gospel, and cinematic orchestration love Ab Major. The key radiates warmth and depth, perfect for emotional, intimate arrangements.

name: Db Major
notes: Db Eb F Gb Ab Bb C
desc: Smooth and mellow, ideal for ballads. Jazz pianists favor Db Major for its pianistic ease and rich tonal palette. R&B slow jams and ballads flourish in this key.

name: A Minor
notes: A B C D E F G
desc: Natural minor shares all notes with C Major but creates darker emotional space. The foundation for rock, pop, and folk. Countless modern hits use this accessible, melancholic key.

name: E Minor
notes: E F# G A B C D
desc: One of rock's pillars. Open Em chord is effortless and sounds raw, emotional, powerful. From indie to metal, E Minor drives countless modern rock songs with its brooding intensity.

name: B Minor
notes: B C# D E F# G A
desc: Dark and contemplative with restless energy. Ubiquitous in modern indie, pop, and electronic music. B Minor has a contemporary feel that resonates with today's songwriters.

name: F# Minor
notes: F# G# A B C# D E
desc: Moody and expressive, the cinematic minor. Metal, alternative rock, and film composers favor F# Minor for its dramatic, intense quality. Haunting and unforgettable.

name: C# Minor
notes: C# D# E F# G# A B
desc: Deep and introspective. Beethoven's Moonlight Sonata resides here. C# Minor demands technical skill and emotional maturity, conveying melancholy and profound introspection.

name: G# Minor
notes: G# A# B C# D# E F#
desc: Intense and brooding, rarely used but unforgettable. The sharp keys intensify emotional darkness. G# Minor has a mysterious, otherworldly quality in progressive metal and art music.

name: D# Minor
notes: D# E# F# G# A# B C#
desc: The darkest of the sharp keys. Enharmonically equivalent to Eb Minor. Extremely rare in practice but theoretically important. Classical composers occasionally used this for maximum dramatic intensity.

name: D Minor
notes: D E F G A Bb C
desc: The saddest key according to Nigel Tufnel. Bach, Mozart, and Beethoven all wrote intensely emotional works here. D Minor exudes nobility, darkness, and classical gravitas.

name: G Minor
notes: G A Bb C D Eb F
desc: Brooding and powerful. Hip-hop, trap, and R&B producers love G Minor's dark, driving groove. The key conveys urgency and raw emotional power in contemporary music.

name: C Minor
notes: C D Eb F G Ab Bb
desc: Deep, dramatic, and emotionally intense. Beethoven's Symphony No. 5 opens with C Minor's defiant cry. This key conveys struggle, passion, and ultimate triumph through darkness.

name: F Minor
notes: F G Ab Bb C Db Eb
desc: Dark and lush, the key of introspection. Jazz ballads, film noir, and R&B soul songs flourish here. F Minor blends sadness with sophistication and emotional depth.

name: Bb Minor
notes: Bb C Db Eb F Gb Ab
desc: Rare but hauntingly beautiful. Deep jazz, art music, and cinematic scoring reach for Bb Minor's uniquely dark, voluminous texture. Complex and sophisticated, for advanced players.
